title: Crab Lab
role_summary: Denver-area act/collective that co-released a split tape with alphabets (2012). Identity and membership not yet confirmed.
body:
# Crab Lab

A musical act or collective that co-released a split tape with alphabets in 2012. The archive folder `alphabets_ Crab Lab split tape` contains one confirmed track: *tienesquelavartelasmanos* (Spanish: "you have to wash your hands"), dated October 23, 2012.

Whether the Spanish-language title was contributed by alphabets or Crab Lab is unknown. The split tape format (each act contributes tracks to a shared release) was common in the cassette/lo-fi DIY scene of the early 2010s.

Nothing further is known about Crab Lab — whether they were a Denver act, a touring band Colin connected with, or a short-lived project. The name suggests a creative/experimental aesthetic consistent with the alphabets scene.

## Archive Notes

- Folder: `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/alphabets_ Crab Lab split tape`
- One MP3 confirmed: *tienesquelavartelasmanos.mp3* (Oct 23, 2012)
- Confidence low: only the folder name and one track provide any information

## Research Gaps

- Who is/was Crab Lab? Members, city, other releases?
- Was the split tape released physically (cassette) or digitally?
- Which tracks on the split are by alphabets and which by Crab Lab?
- What does *tienesquelavartelasmanos* refer to in this context?
